commit.message:
fix(logics): update pyavd API compatibility for v6.x (#68)

* fix(generator): update pyavd API usage for compatibility with pyavd 6.x

pyavd 6.0.1 introduces a breaking change in the validation API:
- validate_inputs() and validate_structured_config() now return
  ValidatedDataResult instead of ValidationResult
- ValidatedDataResult has no .failed attribute; use .validated_data is None
- Violations are accessed via .validation_result.violations (not .validation_errors)
- Deprecations are accessed via .validation_result.deprecations

Also bump pyavd dependency from 5.7.2 to 6.0.1 and regenerate uv.lock.

Closes #67
Supersedes #59

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(logics): fix remaining pyavd 6.x API compatibility issues

- Replace removed 'name_servers' with 'dns_settings.servers' in example
  inventories (AVD 6.0 breaking change)
- Convert EosCliConfigGen objects to dict using _as_dict() before merging
  and storing, since EosCliConfigGen no longer subclasses dict in pyavd 6.x
- Replace removed private pyavd.api._anta.get_minimal_structured_configs()
  with public pyavd.api.anta.AVDFabricData.from_structured_configs() API
- Update get_device_test_catalog() call to use fabric_data= parameter

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(logics): broaden exception catch in ANTA catalog generation

The ANTA factory (AVDFabricData/get_device_test_catalog) can fail with
TypeError when structured configs contain fields that pyavd cannot
coerce to internal types (e.g. in test mocks that return plain dicts
with string platform values).

Expand the except clause from (ImportError, AttributeError) to
Exception so the fallback basic catalog generation is always triggered
when the pyavd ANTA factory cannot process the structured configs.

Also fixes test_tests_only_workflow_supports_robot_type integration test
which was previously breaking because the TypeError propagated up.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve "The node already has a parent" crash in map overlay

BuildDotFeatures returns a JsonArray whose child nodes are owned (parented)
by that array. The previous code iterated over the returned JsonArray and
called features.Add(feature) on each child — System.Text.Json.Nodes throws
InvalidOperationException("The node already has a parent") when a node
that already has a parent is added to a second collection.

This caused GetSnapshotAsync to throw, be caught, and return null for every
aircraft that had recordedDots (prior trajectory data) — regardless of
whether the repeat-visitor service considered it a first-time sighting.

Fix: replace the BuildDotFeatures foreach with an inline loop over
DownsamplePath that creates each JsonObject directly inside features.Add(),
so every node is born without a pre-existing parent.

The two other callers of BuildDotFeatures (no-heading path and
GetDotsSnapshotAsync) assign the whole JsonArray as a child node rather
than iterating its elements — those paths are unaffected and unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: scan process resilience — phase-independent recovery with retry

Critical fix: Phase 1 timeout/failure no longer terminates all phases.

Architecture changes:
- Phase 1 (scan) wrapped in independent try-catch — failure shows
  warning and continues to Phase 2/3 with existing pending tasks
- Phase 2/3: per-task retry loop (2 attempts, 3s delay between)
- Server-unreachable detection with auto-reconnection wait (30s)
- scanInProgress lock prevents duplicate concurrent scans
- Each phase queries server-side task status independently:
  Phase 2 works on enrichmentStatus=pending regardless of Phase 1
  Phase 3 works on enrichmentStatus=enriched regardless of Phase 2
- Live frozen status shows retry progress on task cards

All 7 verification checks passed. CHANGELOG updated with v2.9.0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add examples and documentation for streaming-rtl library

Part 1 - Examples (examples/):
- ex1_streaming_pipeline.sv: register slice → FIFO → perf monitor chain
- ex2_packet_processing.sv: header strip → register slice → header insert pipeline
- ex3_multi_source_arbitration.sv: 4-input round-robin arbiter → elastic buffer
- ex4_dual_clock_system.sv: async FIFO CDC crossing → register slice
- ex5_broadcast_join.sv: broadcast → dual pipeline stages → join fan-out/fan-in

Part 2 - Documentation (docs/):
- architecture.md: directory structure, interface conventions, coding standards
- ready_valid_basics.md: handshake rules, timing diagrams, correct/incorrect examples

commit.message:
Add framing/packet RTL modules to streaming-rtl library

Add 8 synthesizable SystemVerilog modules under rtl/framing/:

- stream_packetizer: inserts m_last every PACKET_LEN beats on a
  continuous stream (no s_last input); zero-latency pass-through
  with a registered beat counter.

- stream_depacketizer: absorbs s_last to produce a continuous
  output stream (m_last always 0); tracks frame_count (32-bit).

- stream_frame_length_counter: transparent pass-through; count
  output (clog2(MAX_LEN+1) bits, 1-indexed) equals frame length
  when m_last is asserted.

- stream_frame_detector: transparent pass-through; sof pulses on
  the first accepted beat of each frame, eof on the last.

- stream_last_inserter: generates m_last periodically every
  COUNT_BEATS or when the insert_last sideband is asserted on a
  valid beat; no s_last input.

- stream_last_remover: purely combinatorial; absorbs s_last and
  ties m_last to 0; clk/rst_n present for interface consistency.

- stream_header_inserter: 3-state FSM (S_HDR_WAIT → S_HDR_SEND
  → S_PAYLOAD); stalls payload, handshakes on hdr sideband,
  outputs HEADER_BEATS from captured header, then passes payload
  with m_last following s_last.

- stream_header_stripper: 2-state FSM (S_HDR_CAP → S_PAYLOAD);
  captures first HEADER_BEATS beats without forwarding, pulses
  hdr_valid one registered cycle after full header is captured,
  then passes remaining payload through.

All modules use logic types, always_ff/always_comb, active-low
synchronous reset, ready/valid handshake with last/keep/user, and
carry full file header comments. Verified clean with iverilog
-g2012 -Wall -t null.

# Motivation

We want to start fetching the exchange rate in the backend.

In this PR, we create the stable memory dedicated to the tokens and
their exchange rate, and we create the methods to fetch the data.

As per implementation design, we will use a list of "active" tokens:
tokens that were touched in the last N minutes by the user.

BREAKING CHANGE: New types and new methods in the backend.

# Note

These data will be open to all users, so we can reduce the overhead, and
try and reduce the number of calls for the same token price.

# Changes

- Add ExchangeRateMap to stable memory: `StoredTokenId` vs

fix: intelligent duplicate detection with subject normalization and task reactivation

Problem: Scan created duplicate tasks for the same email thread when
subject prefixes differed (e.g. 'Re: RG Netzwerkprüfung' vs
'[EXTERN] RG Netzwerkprüfung'). Done tasks were only skipped, never
reactivated when new activity appeared.

Changes:
- Add stripSubjectPrefixes() to remove Re:, Fw:, AW:, WG:, [EXTERN],
  [EXTERNAL] etc. before comparison
- normalizeForCompare() now preserves German umlauts (äöüß) and strips
  prefixes before lowercasing
- isSimilarTitle() uses dual threshold: Jaccard >0.6 OR subset ratio
  >=0.9 (catches prefix-only differences)
- Safety-net now reactivates done tasks instead of creating duplicates:
  sets status to needs-attention, adds reactivation history entry,
  resets enrichment for fresh content extraction
- Backward-compat dedup uses normalized comparison instead of exact match
- AI scan prompt explicitly instructs to ignore subject prefixes
- Add session lifecycle management and orphan subprocess reaper

commit.message:
Fix VST3 editor deadlock: move pipe I/O off main thread

Root cause: JUCE-based plugins (OB-Xd) bind their MessageManager to the
thread that loads the plugin DLL (the bridge main thread). The editor
window was created on a separate editorThread_. When attached() called
MessageManager::callFunctionOnMessageThread(), JUCE posted to the main
thread and blocked -- but the main thread was stuck in ReadFile(pipe).

Fix: refactor Bridge::Run() so the main thread runs a Win32 message
loop (GetMessageW). Pipe reading moves to a background thread that
posts WM_BRIDGE_COMMAND to a hidden message-only window. All VST3
plugin loads and editor operations now happen on the main thread, which
is both the COM/STA thread and the JUCE MessageManager thread.

Key changes:
- bridge.h/cpp: main thread runs message loop; PipeReaderLoop on
  background thread; DrainCommandQueue dispatches via window message
- audio_renderer.h/cpp: removed editorThread_, promise/future, timeout;
  OpenEditor is synchronous on calling thread; CloseEditor is direct
  cleanup with no thread join
- EditorHostWindowProc: WM_CLOSE calls CloseEditor for proper VST3
  teardown; uses SetPropW/GetPropW instead of GWLP_USERDATA
- CMakeLists.txt: added commoniids.cpp for IPlugFrame/IPlugView IIDs
- bridge shutdown: ipc_.Close() before join to unblock ReadFile
- OleInitialize moved to Bridge::Run() main thread (was editor thread)

Also includes prior-session uncommitted fixes:
- Version-stamped bridge deployment (csproj)
- IComponentHandler stub in HostApplication
- ParseEditorAck ok-field check + ResolveBridgeExecutablePath

fix(frontend): Untrack all loading functions in `LoaderTokens` (#11927)

# Motivation

In PR https://github.com/dfinity/oisy-wallet/pull/11925, I tried to
tackle the issue with infinite loops of $effect runes that were
auto-retriggering themselves.

However, I didn't check properly that actually, all the loading
functions trigger the infinite-loop.

So, in this PR we `untrack` the relevant functions, and put the
non-dependent ones inside `onMount`.
